executiveSummary.text: Kneat.com Inc (FOBK) is now back in sync with the canonical DYOR HQ sheet after the metadata repair pass. The current stance is SELL with conviction 28/100, using $2.26 as the latest stored price reference where available.

businessModel.text: Kneat.com Inc provides compliance and validation software to regulated industries, so adoption pace, renewals and execution in enterprise sales matter most.

recentCatalysts.text: New customer wins, upsell into existing accounts and steady subscription growth remain the main catalysts.

thesisEvaluation.text: Base case: the core operating backdrop remains good enough to support the present sell stance, but not strong enough to justify a looser risk view without better evidence. Bull case: execution and demand improve faster than expected. Bear case: operating or funding pressure worsens and compresses valuation further.

keyRisks.text: Small-cap software execution can be uneven. Customer concentration can matter. Long sales cycles can delay growth. Liquidity can be limited. Expectations can move faster than fundamentals.

risks.risk: Small-cap software execution can be uneven.
Long sales cycles can delay growth.
Expectations can move faster than fundamentals.

whoShouldOwn.text: Own: Investors comfortable with smaller-cap compliance software stories. Avoid: Investors needing deep liquidity and mature software margins.

recommendation.text: SELL - Conviction: 28/100. This rebuilt page is intentionally concise, but it is now internally coherent and aligned to the corrected ticker and company metadata.

entryExit.text: Keep position sizing consistent with a sell stance until fuller company-specific analysis is refreshed.
